h2h_summary: The United States hold the overall historical edge with wins in their most recent three friendlies and the only World Cup encounter (3-0 in 1930), though Paraguay won the March 2011 friendly 1-0.

notable_results: USA 3-0 Paraguay - FIFA World Cup Group Stage - 1930

team_style_home: Under Pochettino, the USMNT plays a high-pressing system with attacking fullbacks pushing high in a 4-2-3-1 / 2-3-5 shape, favouring vertical transitions and man-to-man pressing in the middle third.
team_style_away: Alfaro's Paraguay prioritise defensive organisation in a disciplined 4-4-2 block, containing opponents before releasing Almiron quickly in transition on the counter-attack.
wc_history_home: The USA have appeared at 11 World Cups, reaching the semi-finals in 1930 and the quarter-finals in 2002, and are co-hosts of the 2026 edition alongside Canada and Mexico.
wc_history_away: Paraguay are returning to the World Cup for the first time since 2010, when they achieved their best-ever finish by reaching the quarter-finals before losing narrowly 1-0 to eventual champions Spain.
group_context: Group D consists of co-hosts United States, Paraguay, Australia, and Turkiye; the USA face significant home pressure to advance while Paraguay, in their first World Cup in 16 years, are targeting a historic run against a group that offers a realistic path to the knockout stage.
strengths_home: Home crowd advantage in front of a massive partisan support at SoFi Stadium
Individual quality across midfield and attack with Premier League and Serie A regulars
High press and intensity under Pochettino can disrupt less experienced sides
strengths_away: Disciplined defensive structure conceding only 10 goals in 18 qualifying matches
Counter-attacking threat through Almiron's pace and movement in behind defences
Tournament experience in the squad, including veterans of Copa America campaigns
